title: Multi-biome sightings prep for the journal
summary: Twelfth structured packet for Eco Explorer. With the five-biome chain now live and shared species already deduplicated by entry ID, the next journal gap is that a species can appear in more than one biome while save data still records discovery as a single-biome event. This packet carries the scout handoff and the main-agent follow-through for a compact, save-backed multi-biome sightings treatment.

execution_order.goal: Prepare a compact handoff for how multi-biome sightings should appear in the journal once the live five-biome chain exists.
execution_order.why_now: The shared-species data layer already exists, so the next likely friction is player-facing presentation rather than data plumbing.

execution_order.goal: Implement a calm, readable multi-biome sightings treatment in the journal with the small save-model expansion it requires.
execution_order.why_now: The five-biome chain is live, but shared species still read like single-habitat discoveries until repeated sightings can be persisted and surfaced without clutter.

guardrails: Keep the journal discovery-first and avoid turning species pages into checklist dashboards.
Prefer one compact visual treatment over multiple competing badges, chips, and counters.
Do not add more persistent HUD to solve a journal-only communication problem.
Shared species should remain deduplicated by entry ID.
Migrate legacy single-biome discovery saves without wiping progress.
acceptance_focus: The scout handoff should recommend one clear journal pattern for multi-biome sightings plus the data seam that must land first.
The recommended treatment should scale to five biomes inside the existing 192x144 screen language.
The implementation should let repeated shared-species sightings persist on one entry without clutter.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/ecotone-design.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-multi-biome-sightings-handoff.md, and this packet. Implement one save-backed, compact journal treatment for repeated shared-species sightings so entries stay deduplicated by entry ID while the detail pane can show which biomes that species has been seen in.
